chief_complaint: A 17-year-old male presented with persistent fever and headache without focal neurological deficits.
history: The patient had an extensive infectious workup that was inconclusive. He was initially treated with empirical antibiotics without clinical improvement. A repeat cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis demonstrated worsening pleocytosis, prompting an expanded autoimmune and neuroinflammatory panel.
exam: Vital signs: pyrexia. Physical examination: headache.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis: mild pleocytosis. MOG-IgG antibodies detected in both serum and CSF.
